jobs.title: Lead Product Manager
jobs.jd_full:
Who we are:

Want to make an impact? Join our pack and come work (and play!) with us.

We believe everyone deserves the unconditional love of a pet—and at Rover, our mission is to make it easier to experience that love. Founded in 2011, the Rover app and website connect dog and cat parents with loving pet sitters and dog walkers in neighborhoods across the US, Canada, and Europe. We empower our community of trusted pet sitters and dog walkers to run their own pet care businesses on Rover with the tools and security of a global company to back them.

Headquartered in Seattle, Washington, we work closely with our teams in Barcelona, San Antonio, Spokane, and remote locations. We’ve got a reputation for being a great place to work, having been named among the 100 Best Companies to Work For in Seattle Business Magazine and Washington’s Best Workplaces in the Puget Sound Business Journal. We’re an agile, fast-growing company, and our leadership comes from some of the world’s most respected tech companies.

At Rover, our furry coworkers are just as important as our human ones—and we wouldn’t have it any other way. Along with making the joys of pet parenthood more accessible, we’re committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and welcoming community of pet people—and that starts with our employees.

Who we’re looking for:

We are seeking a Senior Product Manager who thrives at the intersection of marketplace economics and monetization innovation. You are a strategic thinker with deep marketplace experience, someone who understands the levers that drive supply-side success and is passionate about building sustainable, high-impact monetization products and value proposition for Rover providers.

In this role, you will lead the development of alternative monetization, provider value added, and economic strategies that strengthen the value proposition for our providers and unlock new revenue streams for Rover. You will work closely with engineering, design, data science, operations, and finance to craft monetization features and economic incentives that balance marketplace health with long-term revenue growth.

The ideal candidate for this role brings a data-driven mindset, business acumen, customer centricity, and a builder’s curiosity, with a proven ability to translate economic principles into impactful product experiences.

This is a hybrid position that has the minimum expectation to work out of our Barcelona Office in the Poblenou neighborhood two days per week on Mondays and Thursdays. For this reason, candidates must be based in Barcelona or willing to relocate.

Qualified candidates will ideally need experience across five key themes:

  • Marketplace expertise: Experience and deep understanding of marketplace dynamics and product strategy. You can navigate ambiguity to design, test, and scale product initiatives that balance value creation and perception across different type of customers,  ensuring both sides of the marketplace feel fairly served while driving sustainable platform growth.ve liquidity, and drive sustainable growth on both sides of the marketplace.
  • Data-driven, customer focused decision making: You are fluent in both quantitative and qualitative insights. You leverage data to uncover opportunity areas, measure elasticity, and evaluate the impact of pricing and monetization tests. At the same time, you build rich customer narratives grounded in qualitative research. You seamlessly balance analytical rigor with customer narratives, translating both numbers and stories into clear marketplace strategies and actionable execution plans.
  • Cross-functional leadership: You excel at bringing together product, engineering, design, analytics, marketing, and finance to define and deliver monetization initiatives. You thrive in ambiguity, communicate effectively across disciplines, and ensure alignment toward shared goals.
  • Execution and iteration mindset: You combine strategic vision with operational excellence. You are able to launch MVPs, measure impact rigorously, and iterate rapidly. You maintain a balance between experimentation speed and quantitative and financial rigor. You also know how to scale products once hypotheses are proven.

Nice to have:

  • Product monetization expertise: Experience identifying and launching new monetization avenues, such as paid premium features, subscription models, tiered services, or monetization of SaaS tooling, that enhance provider success while expanding company revenue.

Plus we are looking for these characteristics:

  • You have a true passion for Rover’s mission and values - and are highly motivated by the responsibility to continuously improve our provider experience and increase revenue growth
  • You have an unlimited curiosity, always probing for the “why” behind user behavior, market shifts, and emerging technologies
  • You know how to leverage qualitative and quantitative data to solve for ambiguous problems, make decisions and drive growth
  • Above all, you seek the truth. You know your market, customers and competition better than anyone, and you excel at integrating this information into your decisions and tactics
  • You can go get the information you need to validate your hypotheses and can synthesize it into what to do next
  • You never hesitate to roll up your sleeves and tackle something hands-on, you persevere when others fall away.
  • You never stop pushing towards clarity and delivery and bring your cross-functional partners along with you.

Your Qualifications:

  • 7+ years of hands-on product experience. Experience in fast-paced environments is a plus.
  • Excellent organizational and analytical skills with strong technical abilities
  • Very comfortable with SQL, Google Analytics, Tableau, Periscope, or other data visualization tools.
  • Experience with multivariate product experimentation frameworks.
  • Experience leading teams through challenging prioritization decisions, creating clear and transparent frameworks that lead to well defined and impactful roadmaps
  • Proven ability to motivate teams to produce quality outcomes within challenging timeframes and ongoing change
  • BS/MS/MBA in Business, Computer Science, Mathematics, Economics or a related technical field or equivalent practical experience
  • Experience working with remote stakeholders, specially engineering, design and data.

Benefits of Working at Rover.com:

  • Long-term incentive plan with a company performance-based cash payout
  • Pension plan
  • Private medical insurance
  • 25 days PTO
  • Meal allowance and flexible compensation plan (transport and nursery)
  • Gym membership
  • €450 to cover the costs associated with the adoption of a pet
  • Annual €150 wellness reimbursement
  • Flexible work hours, sometimes you’ll need to be in at certain times, but on the whole, we’re pretty flexible when it comes to managing workload and time
  • Grab snacks, fresh fruit, in our kitchen to keep yourself going
  • Regular team activities, events, game nights, and more
  • Dog-friendly office

Rover is an equal-opportunity employer committed to promoting a diverse, inclusive, and inventive environment with the best employees. We’re driven by seeing our people succeed and grow, and we work to ensure everyone contributes to their fullest potential. We consider all qualified applicants without regard to age, race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, disability, protected veteran status, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or any other protected status in accordance with applicable laws, regulations, and ordinances.

_

jobs.title: Project Manager
jobs.jd_full:
Company Description

MEDFAR Clinical Solutions was founded in 2010 by two aeronautical engineers who realized that the healthcare system was not exploiting the full potential of technology. Supported by a large community of medical experts and focused on clinical success and patient safety, MEDFAR was the first company to certify a cloud-based Electronic Medical Record in Canada: MYLE (Make Your Life Easy).

Committed to promoting excellence and effectiveness in healthcare worldwide, MEDFAR differentiates itself by offering a unique healthcare management solution for clinics, which replaces inefficient processes with a faster and safer technological alternative.

Job Description

The deployment of the MYLE solution is at the heart of MEDFAR’s operations. The project manager’s role is to prepare, plan, and coordinate projects. They will be responsible for managing schedules, monitoring progress, and reporting results for their project portfolio. Their contribution to the deployment team will have a direct and significant impact on improving clinical processes. The project manager is a catalyst. He or she brings projects to fruition and coordinates all stakeholders involved in the project, whether through its implementation or its outcome. He or she manages the impact of the project, both during its implementation and after its completion.

The project manager is part of a multidisciplinary team whose mandate is to welcome new clients in the implementation of our care management solutions. The project manager coordinates the implementation of complex projects with multiple stakeholders. He or she is also responsible for guiding team members in achieving the unit’s objectives.

Primary responsibilities

  • Ensure rigorous planning, coordination, and monitoring of deployment projects in accordance with established schedules, budgets, and requirements.
  • Define and assign tasks to team members based on their skills and availability.
  • Regularly communicate project progress to internal and external stakeholders
  • Proactively identify and manage project risks, challenges, and change requests.
  • Ensure compliance with applicable project management standards, processes, and methodologies.
  • Ensure the quality of deliverables and customer satisfaction throughout deployments
  • Work closely with technical, product, and support teams to ensure a smooth transition.
  • Depending on the size of the projects under his responsibility, he plans and coordinates several projects simultaneously according to the life cycle of each one.
  • Evaluates the motivations, objectives, and success criteria of the project for the client;
  • Establishes an implementation plan and schedule for each upcoming project and evaluates objectives;
  • Responsible for the performance of the portfolio of projects included in his team
  • Analysis of workshops and proposals for improving clinical processes;
  • Organizes and leads technical meetings with stakeholders when interface adjustments are required;
  • The project manager is authorized to collaborate with multiple stakeholders and demonstrate mastery of political skills.
  • Manages the resources assigned to his or her team.

Qualifications

Contribute with your strengths:

  • University degree in project management, computer science, a health-related discipline, or a related field
  • 5 to 7 years of successful experience in managing large-scale deployment projects
  • Excellent leadership, team management, and resource development skills
  • Bilingual
  • Strong organizational skills;
  • Proven experience in stakeholder management and conflict resolution
  • Excellent customer service, thoroughness, commitment to excellence, and attention to detail;
  • Strong oral and written communication skills
  • Ability to work under pressure and manage multiple projects simultaneously
  • Knowledge of the health and social services sector is an asset;
  • PMP, CSM, or other project management certification is an asset;

Working conditions

  • Car travel varies depending on the week;
  • High degree of flexibility in terms of schedule and availability to travel within the region or to other provinces.
  • Location: Montreal
  • Permanent full-time position (40 hours per week)
  • Hybrid work model
  • Languages: Fluency in French and English, both written and spoken, is required.

Additional Information

What’s in it for you?

By joining MEDFAR, you will be part of a purpose-driven organization whose product is disrupting and digitizing health care, making doctors’ and healthcare workers’ lives easier, while enabling better patient health outcomes. Becoming a MEDFARian also means having the opportunity to be part of an innovative community and working in a dynamic environment where your work will have a meaningful and tangible effect not only on your team and the business but most importantly on the healthcare industry.

  • Remote work and flexibility (supporting work-life balance)
  • RRSP contribution
  • Healthcare insurance from day one
  • Paid time off: 3 weeks + 1 additional week between Christmas and New Year
  • Annual training allowance ($1,500) to support your professional development
  • An onboarding program to help you get familiar with our environment and the digital healthcare field
  • All IT equipment is provided, with additional gear if needed
  • Internal growth opportunities (promotions, internal mobility)
  • Support from a wellness and social committee, with initiatives to foster team cohesion, mental health, and employee well-being
  • A company culture focused on transparency, collaboration, and innovation

Join a dynamic and innovative environment where your work has a real and wide-reaching impact, helping to modernize healthcare in Canada and internationally.

With offices around the world, fluency in both French and English is a must at MEDFAR. Because of the need to communicate with colleagues and/or customers in other provinces or countries, bilingualism enables us to communicate in both languages while promoting the use of French.

At MEDFAR, we value diversity, equity and inclusion within our team. We are committed to providing a work environment where every individual feels respected and supported, regardless of their background, identity or abilities.As part of our commitment to a fair and inclusive recruitment process, we offer accommodation to candidates who request it. If you need accommodation during your interview, please let us know so that we can provide you with an adapted experience.

MEDFAR has voluntarily subscribed to an Equal Employment Opportunity Program (EEOP). We encourage applications from women, visible minorities, ethnic minorities, aboriginal peoples and people with disabilities. When applying, we invite you to complete this section, which enables us to implement our Equal Employment Opportunity Program (EEOP). Self-identification is not compulsory, but may enable you to benefit from hiring or promotion measures if you have the skills required for the job.

To better understand the self-identification process, please consult this guide.

jobs.title: Senior Product Manager Data Platform & Products
jobs.jd_full:
Company Description

Visa is a world leader in payments technology, facilitating transactions between consumers, merchants, financial institutions and government entities across more than 200 countries and territories, dedicated to uplifting everyone, everywhere by being the best way to pay and be paid.

At Visa, you’ll have the opportunity to create impact at scale — tackling meaningful challenges, growing your skills and seeing your contributions impact lives around the world. Join Visa and do work that matters — to you, to your community, and to the world.

Progress starts with you.

Job Description

The Visa Acceptance Platform team is seeking a Senior Product Manager to join the Acceptance Data Platform & Analytics organization. This team builds and operates a modern, cloud‑native data platform that enables Visa Acceptance products and domains to own, manage, and scale their data and analytics capabilities, while leveraging shared platform services for consistency, governance, and operational efficiency.

The Acceptance Data Platform serves as the foundational layer for analytics, reporting, KPIs, and downstream intelligence across Acceptance products. While the platform increasingly supports AI‑enabled use cases, this role is first and foremost focused on data platform capabilities—including data ingestion, modeling, metrics standardization, APIs, governance, and analytics enablement at scale.

We are looking for a Senior Product Manager with strong data platform and analytics expertise, who can partner deeply with data engineering and platform teams to deliver reliable, governed, and reusable data capabilities that power decision‑making across the Acceptance ecosystem.

What You Will Do:

In this role, you will own the vision, strategy, and execution of the Acceptance Data Platform, ensuring it provides scalable, reliable, and well‑governed data foundations for analytics and reporting across Visa Acceptance products. You will work closely with data engineering, architecture, and analytics teams to define and prioritize cloud‑native data platform capabilities such as data ingestion, modeling, KPI and metrics standardization, APIs, and governance, while balancing domain flexibility with enterprise standards. You will enable consistent, trusted analytics and self‑service reporting for product teams, operational users, and leadership, and ensure the platform scales efficiently with strong performance and cost controls. As a secondary focus, you will ensure the data platform is AI‑ready by delivering high‑quality, well‑modeled data and standardized metrics that can be safely consumed by machine learning and AI platforms. Throughout, you will drive cross‑functional delivery, measure success through adoption and data quality metrics, and continuously improve the platform based on telemetry and stakeholder feedback

How the Day-to-Day Work Looks Like:

In this role, your day‑to‑day work centers on building and evolving the Acceptance Data Platform that powers analytics, reporting, and downstream intelligence across Visa Acceptance solutions. You operate at the intersection of product strategy, data platforms, analytics, and enablement, ensuring the foundation is scalable, governed, and trusted.

On a typical day, you will:

  • Work closely with data engineering and platform teams to define and prioritize core data platform capabilities—such as ingestion, storage, data modeling, KPI standardization, APIs, and reporting frameworks—balancing scalability, performance, cost, and governance.
  • Translate complex platform and analytics needs into clear product requirements, maintaining a well‑prioritized backlog that engineering teams can execute against with confidence.
  • Review and validate data schemas, KPI definitions, and analytics designs to ensure metrics are consistent, reusable, and trusted across Acceptance products.
  • Partner with domain product teams and analytics consumers to understand how data is used today, identify gaps or duplication, and introduce shared platform capabilities that accelerate delivery and reduce fragmentation.
  • Guide the evolution of analytics and reporting experiences, ensuring dashboards, APIs, and self‑service tools are built on standardized data and platform‑defined metrics rather than one‑off implementations.
  • Collaborate with architecture, security, privacy, and governance teams to ensure data platform capabilities meet compliance, quality, and auditability requirements without slowing innovation.
  • Enrich and strengthen the Acceptance Solutions Machine Learning Platform (MLP) and Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP) by:
    • Ensuring high‑quality, well‑modeled, and well‑governed data is available as input for models and AI workflows
    • Driving alignment between data platform capabilities and MLP/AIP requirements for feature consistency, reproducibility, and operational readiness
    • Partnering with ML and AI teams to identify platform improvements that simplify model training, deployment, and consumption—without shifting ownership of model logic into the data platform
  • Use platform telemetry, adoption metrics, and stakeholder feedback to continuously refine priorities and guide roadmap decisions.
  • Participate in agile delivery rituals to ensure predictable, high‑quality execution across multiple engineering teams.
  • Communicate platform progress, trade‑offs, and outcomes clearly to stakeholders and leadership, reinforcing trust in the data and analytics foundation.

This is a hybrid position. Expectation of days in office will be confirmed by your hiring manager.

Qualifications

Required Qualifications:

  • 8 or more years of relevant work experience with a Bachelor Degree or at least 5 years of experience with an Advanced Degree (e.g. Masters, MBA, JD, MD) or 2 years of work experience with a PhD

Preferred Qualifications:

  • 9 or more years of relevant work experience with a Bachelor Degree or 7 or more relevant years of experience with an Advanced Degree (e.g. Masters, MBA, JD, MD) or 3 or more years of experience with a PhD
  • Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Information Systems, or a related field
  • Experience in Payment Processing domain
  • Proven experience owning and delivering complex data platform or analytics products
  • Hands on experience with cloud native data platforms (e.g., Amazon S3, Databricks, ClickHouse, or similar)
  • Familiarity with data mesh concepts, federated governance, or domain oriented data ownership
  • Experience building platform products consumed by multiple internal teams
  • Exposure to AI enabled analytics or ML use cases (as a consumer of data platforms, not as a core focus)
  • Experience working in large scale, regulated, enterprise environments

Strong understanding of:

  • Data modeling, schemas, and metrics design
  • Analytics and reporting platforms
  • How data quality and governance impact downstream use cases
  • Ability to partner effectively with senior data engineering and platform teams
  • Excellent communication, problem solving, and decision making skills in complex, ambiguous environments

Additional Information

Work Hours: Varies upon the needs of the department.

Travel Requirements: This position requires travel 5-10% of the time.

Mental/Physical Requirements: This position will be performed in an office setting.  The position will require the incumbent to sit and stand at a desk, communicate in person and by telephone, frequently operate standard office equipment, such as telephones and computers.

Visa is an EEO Employer.  Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability or protected veteran status.  Visa will also consider for employment qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with EEOC guidelines and applicable local law.

Visa will consider for employment qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with applicable local law, including the requirements of Article 49 of the San Francisco Police Code.

U.S. APPLICANTS ONLY: The estimated salary range for this position is 152,200.00 to 243,700.00 USD per year, which may include potential sales incentive payments (if applicable). Salary may vary depending on job-related factors which may include knowledge, skills, experience, and location. In addition, this position may be eligible for bonus and equity. Visa has a comprehensive benefits package for which this position may be eligible that includes Medical, Dental, Vision, 401 (k), FSA/HSA, Life Insurance, Paid Time Off, and Wellness Program.

jobs.title: Senior Platform Technical Product Manager
jobs.jd_full:
About Invoca

Invoca is the leading AI-powered conversation intelligence platform, enabling marketing, sales, customer experience, and contact center teams to understand and act on the intent behind every customer interaction. We reveal the intelligence within phone calls, texts, and chats, helping businesses drive better customer experiences, increase revenue, and operate more efficiently. We are a fast-growing company with a collaborative culture and a deep commitment to building reliable, transparent, and high-impact Data and AI systems.The Opportunity: Architecting Invoca’s Platform Foundation

As Invoca accelerates the modernization of its platform—moving from a monolith to a service-oriented architecture leveraging IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS solutions—we are adopting a Platform as a Service strategy. This is a high-impact role where you will be instrumental in building the scalable, reliable, and widely-adopted technical foundations for our entire engineering organization. Your success will directly translate into improved engineering velocity, reduced technical debt, and clear alignment between platform investments and core business outcomes.About the Team

You will be a key member of the Architecture team, partnering directly with the Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) and Developer Enablement teams.

  • Developer Enablement: Builds, maintains, and supports the tooling, gems, libraries, CI/CD, and foundational services (like Identity) that all Invoca services rely on.
  • SRE: Provides the core infrastructure, observability, and stability to enable engineering teams to build new services and scale our platform effectively.

Your Role and Impact

We are seeking a Platform Technical Product Manager (TPM) to define the strategy, roadmap, and execution for our internal platform capabilities. This role requires a systems mindset, sitting at the intersection of engineering, architecture, and product to build scalable, reliable, and reusable platform services that power our product ecosystem.

You will operate as a strategic individual contributor, reporting to the Sr. Director of Architecture, and partner closely with Engineering, Infrastructure, Security, Data, and Product teams to ensure our platform supports rapid innovation, high system reliability, and long-term scalability.What You’ll Be Doing (Key Responsibilities)

  • Platform Strategy & Vision: Define and drive the vision, strategy, and roadmap for core platform services (e.g., APIs, infrastructure, identity, observability, internal tooling), ensuring platform investments directly align with company-level product and business goals.
  • Technical Debt & Velocity: Proactively identify and champion opportunities to reduce technical debt, improve platform scalability, and accelerate overall development velocity across the organization.
  • Cross-Functional Leadership: Partner with engineering leaders to define architectural direction and platform standards. Collaborate with all stakeholders to understand platform dependencies and drive alignment across multiple engineering teams.
  • Roadmap & Execution: Translate platform strategy into clear, prioritized roadmaps and technical requirements (product briefs, epics, user stories). Define and track success metrics, including SLAs, performance benchmarks, and internal adoption rates.
  • Technical Decision-Making: Evaluate complex build vs. borrow vs. buy decisions and vendor integrations. Participate in architectural reviews and design discussions, leveraging a deep understanding of distributed systems, cloud infrastructure (AWS/GCP), and data architectures.
  • Developer Experience: Ensure platform services are well-documented, discoverable, and user-friendly. Actively gather internal customer feedback to continuously improve the overall developer experience and track platform adoption across engineering teams.

Core Qualifications (What You’ll Need)

We are looking for a proven track record of impact and experience, recognizing that this is not always tied to a strict number of years.

  • Experience & Impact:

    • 5+ years of hands-on experience in a platform, infrastructure, or other engineering-heavy role, which includes 2+ years of dedicated Product Management experience. (Equivalent experience leading complex technical initiatives is highly valued).
    • Proven track record of successfully leading and delivering complex, cross-team technical initiatives in an Agile/Scrum environment.
    • Exceptional prioritization and decision-making skills with an analytical, data-driven approach.

  • Technical Fluency:

    • Strong working knowledge of distributed systems, APIs, cloud infrastructure (AWS/GCP), and data platforms.
    • Familiarity with CI/CD, DevOps practices, and system reliability principles.
    • Ability to read technical documentation and engage credibly in architecture-level design discussions.

  • Soft Skills & Leadership:

    • Demonstrated ability to influence without direct authority across multiple technical teams.
    • Strong written and verbal communication skills, able to articulate technical trade-offs to both engineers and business stakeholders.

Bonus Experience (Great to Have)

  • Experience building platforms in high-scale or enterprise SaaS environments.
  • Background in software engineering or technical architecture.
  • Experience with observability, identity, security, or internal developer platforms.
  • Familiarity with AI/ML infrastructure or data-intensive systems.
  • Experience supporting enterprise-grade security and compliance standards.

Salary, Benefits & Perks:

At Invoca, all new hires in the U.S. receive benefits starting on day one of employment. Our benefits offerings include:

Please note that benefits for teammates outside the U.S. may vary in accordance with their country’s laws and regulations.

  • Flexible Time Off – We encourage a healthy work-life balance. Our flexible paid time off policy allows you to recharge and take time away as needed.
  • Paid Holidays – Invoca provides 16 U.S. paid holidays, including a winter break, giving you ample opportunity to refresh and spend time with friends and family.
  • Health Benefits – Our healthcare program includes medical, dental, and vision coverage, with multiple plan options so you can choose what works best for you and your family. Fertility assistance is also included.
  • Retirement – Invoca offers a 401(k) plan through Fidelity with a company match of up to 4%.
  • Stock Options – All employees are invited to share in Invoca’s success through stock options.
  • Mental Health Program– Well-being support on a broad range of issues is available through our SpringHealth program.
  • Paid Family Leave – Up to 6 weeks of 100% paid leave is provided for baby bonding, adoption, and caring for family members.
  • Paid Medical Leave – Up to 12 weeks of 100% paid leave is provided for childbirth and medical needs.
  • InVacation – As a thank-you to our long-term team members, we offer a bonus after 7 years of service.
  • Wellness Subsidy – We provide a subsidy that can be applied toward gym memberships, fitness classes, and more.
  • Position Base Range - Salary Range $117,000-$175,000 / plus bonus potential

Diversity, Equity & Inclusion

Invoca is committed to building an inclusive culture that values diverse perspectives and experiences. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer

#LI-Remote

jobs.title: Senior Product Manager Endpoint
jobs.jd_full:
Company Description

Nexthink is the leader in digital employee experience management software. The company provides IT leaders with unprecedented insight allowing them to see, diagnose and fix issues at scale impacting employees anywhere, with any application or network, before employees notice the issue. As the first solution to allow IT to progress from reactive problem solving to proactive optimization, Nexthink enables its more than 1,300 customers to provide better digital experiences to more than 18 million employees. Dual headquartered in Lausanne, Switzerland and Boston, Massachusetts, Nexthink has 9 offices worldwide.

#LI-Hybrid

Job Description

We are looking for a (Senior) Product Manager to define and execute the roadmap for a core part of Nexthink’s endpoint troubleshooting capabilities.

This role sits at the intersection of deep platform work and customer-facing product experiences. It focuses on improving how endpoint issues are detected, understood, and resolved at scale, enabling better L1 to L3 troubleshooting workflows and faster issue resolution. The role requires strong technical depth and product judgment, with the ability to work across data, integrations, backend systems, and user workflows while staying focused on customer value. You will collaborate closely with engineering, architecture, design, and customer-facing teams to turn complex technical challenges into clear, high-impact product capabilities.

Your responsibilities will be:

  • Define and drive the roadmap for endpoint troubleshooting capabilities, aligned with customer needs, business priorities, and platform direction.
  • Lead delivery of capabilities that improve how endpoint issues are detected, understood, and resolved.
  • Translate complex technical topics into clear product requirements, priorities, and trade-offs.
  • Work closely with engineering, architecture, and design to build reliable, scalable, and intuitive solutions.
  • Go deep into endpoint-related technical subjects, including APIs, integrations, data flows, and backend dependencies, to support sound product decisions.
  • Identify opportunities to apply AI in practical ways that improve troubleshooting effectiveness and user experience.
  • Partner with support, field, and customer-facing teams to understand pain points and turn them into product opportunities.
  • Define and track metrics to measure adoption, troubleshooting effectiveness, and product impact.

Qualifications

  • Master’s degree in computer science, engineering, or equivalent practical experience, with a strong technical foundation.
  • 3+ years of experience as a Product Manager in a product-led company, ideally in enterprise software or another technical domain.
  • Strong ability to understand complex technical systems and work closely with engineering teams while staying focused on customer value and outcomes.
  • Solid understanding of platform architecture, APIs, integrations, backend systems, and endpoint-related technical concepts.
  • Good understanding of data-related product topics, including data collection, quality, structure, and how data supports troubleshooting workflows and product capabilities.
  • Strong interest in AI-driven product experiences, with familiarity in how AI can be applied to troubleshooting, data-driven workflows, and product capabilities, and good judgment on where it creates real value.
  • Ability to think critically about how emerging AI methods can improve user experience, automation, and product effectiveness in practical, scalable ways.
  • Able to operate effectively in a fast-paced environment with changing priorities, clear trade-offs, and disciplined choices about what not to build.
  • Professional-level English, spoken and written.

Nice to have

  • Experience with endpoint technologies, device troubleshooting, or observability-related products.
  • Experience with L3 support, root cause analysis, or incident resolution workflows.
  • Working knowledge of ITSM tools and practices such as ServiceNow or BMC Remedy.
  • Exposure to customer-facing roles such as support, pre-sales, post-sales, or customer success.

Additional Information

We are the pioneers and trailblazers of a global IT Market Category (DEX) that is shaping the future of how the world works, giving our customers’ IT Teams total digital visibility across their enterprise. Our innovative solutions integrate real-time analytics, automation, and employee feedback across all endpoints. This enables our IT teams to solve complex technical challenges, create ever more productive workplaces, and deliver happy, satisfied employees in the digital workplace.

With over 1000 employees across 5 continents, Nexthink operates as One Team, connecting, collaborating and innovating to continuously grow. We call our employees ‘Nexthinkers’ and our commitment to diversity, inclusion, and equity is second to none. We currently have over 75 nationalities working with us, from all cultures and backgrounds, speaking many different languages.

IIf you are looking for a change and like a nice atmosphere, lots of challenges, and having fun while working, this is a great opportunity for you! Check what we offer:

  • 💼 Permanent Contract and a competitive compensation package.
  • 📍 Beautiful office, conveniently located next to the Prilly-Malley train station
  • 🏡 Hybrid work model balancing office and remote work, with a structured approach for new hires to foster connections and onboarding.
  • 🏖️ Flexible Hours and unlimited vacation (employees have unlimited paid time off on top of the 25 days of holidays we offer) plus 3 company-paid volunteer days.
  • 🤸 Free access to a fitness centre inside the building.
  • 🚞 Reimbursement of the half-fare travel card for public transport.
  • 🧑‍🏫 Reimbursement up to 50% of the cost of French classes.
  • 🍉 Fresh fruit, cookies, and soft drinks as well.
  • 🤝 Regular company and team events like Voluntary Days, Pizza talks, Team Building activities, hosting Meetups at the office and more!
  • 📣  Bonuses for referring successful hires after three months of continuous employment.
  • 🚚 We offer a relocation package to people who are coming from another country.

Please note that not all the benefits listed above are available for temporary, contract, and internship roles. To ensure you have the most up-to-date information, we recommend checking with your Recruitment Partner

jobs.title: Product Manager
jobs.jd_full:
Company Description

Bosch Home Comfort in Tranås develops, manufactures, and sells advanced heat pumps that harness energy from nature’s own resources – rock, water, and air.

In Tranås, you’ll find both our production unit and our competence and development center, where we constantly strive to stay at the forefront . Here we create smart solutions that simplify everyday life, contribute to a greener future, and provide our customers with safety and comfort

We believe in technology that makes a difference – technology that is Invented for life.

Job Description

Your contribution to something big

As a Nordic Product manager at Bosch Home Comfort you will be responsible for the Nordic high output/Commercial Heat pump Portfolio. You will also be responsible for maintaining products, as well as new upcoming projects for the Nordic countries.

  • Nordic heat pump portfolio ownership

You will manage and develop the Nordic commercial product portfolio for heat pumps, including air-to-water systems, and geothermal solutions.

  • Product development and updates for all Nordic markets

You are responsible for ensuring continuous product improvements, updates, and lifecycle management across the entire Nordic region.

  • Balanced strategic and operational responsibilities

The role combines long-term strategic planning with hands-on operational work, with an approximate split of 60% strategic and 40% operational tasks depending on project timing and needs

  • Compliance with Bosch standards

You ensure that all product-related processes, documentation, and quality standards are fully aligned with Bosch requirements.

The position can be placed both in Tranås, Sweden or our location in Copenhagen, Denmark.

Qualifications

What distinguishes you:

  • University degree, preferably a Master’s in a relevant field; alternatively, extensive and relevant professional experience may compensate for formal education.
  • Strong knowledge of heat pump and/or refrigeration technology, with a particular focus on commercial and property heat pump systems.
  • Previous professional experience working with heat pumps, preferably within product management, technical support, sales, or a similar field.
  • Fluent in English as well as either Swedish or Danish

Preferred skills:

  • Previous experience within the heat pump industry.
  • Strategic experience within the heat pump sector, including a solid understanding of installation requirements and processes.
  • Prior work experience with Bosch is considered an advantage.

Personal Qualities

You are a structured and organized professional with a strong ability to maintain an overview and see the bigger picture. Your commercial mindset enables you to make decisions that support the long-term success of the business. You communicate clearly and effectively, and your strong interpersonal skills make you both pedagogical and approachable. Overall, you thrive in collaborative environments and contribute positively through clear communication, analytical thinking, and a well-grounded commercial understanding.

Additional Information

Welcome to Tranås – the heart of Bosch Home Comfort in Sweden

Tranås, beautifully located by the idyllic Lake Sommen and surrounded by forests and nature reserves, is home to Bosch Thermotechnology AB in Sweden. Here, more than 600 associates work in manufacturing and research & development of innovative heating solutions for a sustainable future.

At Bosch, we care about you, our business, and the environment.

We offer more than a job – we help you grow in your career and as a person. Life is about balance, and we support your goals, whether they involve new challenges, development, or time for family and hobbies.

Join a workplace that values individuality, openness, and trust, where you can be yourself and be inspired by diverse perspectives from around the globe.

Join us in Tranås or Copnehagen and be part of shaping tomorrow’s energy solutions – in a workplace where innovation meets quality of life.

Here, you’ll find not just a job – but a lifestyle.

Compensation and Benefits

  • Competitive salary, Pension plans, health insurance, and other benefits according to the collective bargin.
  • Flexible working hours and hybrid work options (depending on role).
  • Employee discounts on Bosch products.

jobs.title: Product Manager
jobs.jd_full:
About AlayaCare

At AlayaCare, we’re more than just a fast-growing SaaS company — we’re a team of people passionate about transforming home healthcare. Our cloud-based platform empowers care providers around the world to deliver better outcomes for their clients.

With 650+ employees across Canada, the US, Australia, and Brazil, we’re united by a shared mission and a strong culture of transparency, growth, and human connection. Whether you’re early in your career or a seasoned expert, AlayaCare offers the opportunity to grow your impact, your skills, and your career.

About the Role

Reporting to the Director of Data Engineering, the Product Manager, Data owns the strategy and success of our embedded analytics offering, Data Exploration. You will lead the product vision and roadmap, shaping how customers access, understand, and leverage data to drive better outcomes.

In this role, you’ll play a key part in building scalable, intuitive data capabilities that are deeply embedded into our product experience. You’ll balance customer needs, technical complexity, and business priorities to deliver solutions that are both impactful and easy to use.

This is a highly cross-functional role that requires strong product leadership, a customer-first mindset, and a solid foundation in data management. You’ll collaborate across teams to deliver high-impact data solutions for our customers.

What You’ll Do

Product Success

  • Own the success of Data Exploration, balancing priorities, managing stakeholders, and fostering innovation
  • Conduct market research and competitive analysis to identify opportunities for feature enhancements and new capabilities

Customer Focus & Point of Contact

  • Serve as the main representative for Data Exploration, engaging with clients, partners, and internal teams
  • Address escalated concerns or complex requirements efficiently

Data Governance

  • Define roles and responsibilities for data stewardship, ownership, and accuracy
  • Manage metadata processes: document pipelines, maintain a data dictionary, and establish a business glossary
  • Oversee data governance tools and ensure integration with workflows
  • Promote data literacy through training, demos, and enablement

Feature Development & Engineering Collaboration

  • Partner with engineering to define feature requirements and deliver on scope and timelines
  • Use analytics and user feedback to guide decisions and track KPIs
  • Prioritize and manage the product backlog, facilitating agile processes as needed

Strategic Leadership & Innovation

  • Define and communicate product strategy, positioning, and messaging
  • Proactively identify challenges and propose solutions
  • Drive the product vision forward, influencing leadership and cross-functional partners

What You Bring to the Team

  • Technical background with experience in software development or data management
  • Strong problem-solving skills, attention to detail, and a growth mindset in a changing environment
  • Experience gathering and interpreting product data and customer feedback to inform decisions
  • Strong cross-functional collaboration skills with engineering, product design, support, and sales
  • Ability to maintain strong customer relationships and provide product support
  • Strong ability to analyze industry trends and competitive landscape
  • Competency in project reporting, risk management, and status tracking
  • Experience training internal teams on new product features and promoting data literacy
  • Agile/Scrum certification or experience working in agile environments.
  • Experience in healthcare or related industries is as asset
  • Bilingual proficiency in French and English is an asset.
  • Data management certifications (e.g., CDMP, DGSP, CIPP) are an asset

Why Join AlayaCare?

Work With Purpose

At AlayaCare, you’ll help build technology that empowers care providers and improves outcomes for patients and families. Every line of code and every product decision contributes to making care more connected, accessible, and human.

Grow in a High-Trust Culture

We believe in transparency, feedback, and assuming positive intent. You’ll be supported through mentorship, career mobility, and a strong promote-from-within philosophy.

Balance That Works for You

From flexible vacation to Wellness Fridays and paid volunteer time off, we prioritize well-being and sustainable performance.

Benefits That Matter

  • Competitive compensation, including equity in a well-funded, scaling company

  • Comprehensive group benefits program, including telemedicine, effective on your first day

  • Employee expense program for health, wellness, lifestyle, and productivity expenses

  • Parental leave top-up plan

  • Flexible vacation policy

  • Paid Volunteer Time Off program

  • Career growth and development opportunities

Inclusive by Design

We celebrate diverse perspectives and foster belonging through our DEIB initiatives. Employee-led events, summits, and social activities — both in-person and virtual — create meaningful connections across our global teams.

Location and Work Model

This role is based in the Greater Montreal Area. At AlayaCare, our hybrid model includes set in-office collaboration days, and team members are expected to be present in the office on those days to foster connection, innovation, and teamwork.

Ready to Join Us?

Apply today and be part of a company that’s shaping the future of home and community care. Not the right role for you? Share this posting with someone who might be a great fit.

AlayaCare uses AI tools during our hiring process to support fair, consistent, and objective decision-making. Some initial screening steps may be automated to help identify qualified candidates. If your application is declined automatically, you may request a human review.

We’re committed to creating a workplace where everyone belongs. If you require accommodation during the application process, please reach out to careers@alayacare.com
